Termination of Advisory CouncilsAdvisory councils established after , to terminate not later than the expiration of the 2-year period beginning on the date of their establishment, unless, in the case of a council established by the President or an officer of the Federal Government, such council is renewed by appropriate action prior to the expiration of such 2-year period, or in the case of a council established by the Congress, its duration is otherwise provided by law. Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–208, div. B, title I, § 101110 Stat. 3009–523Sept. 30, 1996“This title [see Codification note above] may be cited as the ‘Opal Creek Wilderness and Opal Creek Scenic Recreation Area Act of 1996’.”

children.heading: Bull of the Woods Wilderness
children.label: 1 Bull of the Woods Wilderness
children.text: The term “Bull of the Woods Wilderness” means the land designated as wilderness by section 3(4) of the Oregon Wilderness Act of 1984 (; note).Public Law 98–32816 U.S.C. 1132

children.heading: Opal Creek Wilderness
children.label: 2 Opal Creek Wilderness
children.text: The term “Opal Creek Wilderness” means certain land in the Willamette National Forest in the State of Oregon comprising approximately 12,800 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed Opal Creek Wilderness and Scenic Recreation Area”, dated July 1996.

children.heading: Scenic Recreation Area
children.label: 3 Scenic Recreation Area
children.text: The term “Scenic Recreation Area” means the Opal Creek Scenic Recreation Area, comprising approximately 13,000 acres, as generally depicted on the map entitled “Proposed Opal Creek Wilderness and Scenic Recreation Area”, dated July 1996 and established under subsection (c)(1)(C).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 4 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Agriculture.

to establish a wilderness and scenic recreation area to protect and provide for the enhancement of the natural, scenic, recreational, historic and cultural resources of the area in the vicinity of Opal Creek;
to protect and support the economy of the communities of the Santiam Canyon; and
to provide increased protection for an important drinking water source for communities served by the North Santiam River.

children.label: A
children.text: the Opal Creek Wilderness, as depicted on the map described in subsection (a)(2), is hereby designated as wilderness, subject to the provisions of the Wilderness Act [ et seq.], shall become a component of the National Wilderness System, and shall be known as the Opal Creek Wilderness;16 U.S.C. 1131

children.label: B
children.text: the part of the Bull of the Woods Wilderness that is located in the Willamette National Forest shall be incorporated into the Opal Creek Wilderness; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Secretary shall establish the Opal Creek Scenic Recreation Area in the Willamette National Forest in the State of Oregon, comprising approximately 13,000 acres, as generally depicted on the map described in subsection (a)(3).

children.label: I
children.text: Santiam Number 1, mineral survey number 992, as described in patent number 39–92–0002, dated ;December 11, 1991

children.label: II
children.text: Ruth Quartz Mine Number 2, mineral survey number 994, as described in patent number 39–91–0012, dated ;February 12, 1991

children.label: III
children.text: Morning Star Lode, mineral survey number 993, as described in patent number 36–91–0011, dated ;February 12, 1991

children.label: ii
children.text: all right, title, and interest held by any entity other than the Times Mirror Land and Timber Company, its successors and assigns, in and to lands located in section 18, township 8 south, range 5 east, Marion County, Oregon, Eureka numbers 6, 7, 8, and 13 mining claims; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an easement across the Hewitt, Starvation, and Poor Boy Mill Sites, mineral survey number 990, as described in patent number 36–91–0017, dated . In the sole discretion of the Secretary, such easement may be limited to administrative use if an alternative access route, adequate and appropriate for public use, is provided.May 9, 1991

children.label: i
children.text: The lode mining claims known as Princess Lode, Black Prince Lode, and King Number 4 Lode, embracing portions of sections 29 and 32, township 8 south, range 5 east, Willamette Meridian, Marion County, Oregon, the claims being more particularly described in the field notes and depicted on the plat of mineral survey number 887, Oregon; and

children.label: ii
children.text: Ruth Quartz Mine Number 1, mineral survey number 994, as described in patent number 39–91–0012, dated .February 12, 1991

children.heading: Additions to the wilderness and scenic recreation areas
children.label: 3 Additions to the wilderness and scenic recreation areas
A
children.text: Lands or interests in lands conveyed to the United States under this subsection shall be included in and become part of, as appropriate, Opal Creek Wilderness or the Opal Creek Scenic Recreation Area.

children.label: B
children.text: On acquiring all or substantially all of the land located in section 36, township 8 south, range 4 east, of the Willamette Meridian, Marion County, Oregon, commonly known as the Rosboro section by exchange, purchase from a willing seller, or by donation, the Secretary shall expand the boundary of the Scenic Recreation Area to include such land.

children.label: C
children.text: On acquiring all or substantially all of the land located in section 18, township 8 south, range 5 east, Marion County, Oregon, commonly known as the Time Mirror property, by exchange, purchase from a willing seller, or by donation, such land shall be included in and become a part of the Opal Creek Wilderness.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall administer the Scenic Recreation Area in accordance with this section and the laws (including regulations) applicable to the National Forest System.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of establishment of the Scenic Recreation Area, the Secretary, in consultation with the advisory committee established under subsection (e)(1), shall prepare a comprehensive Opal Creek Management Plan (Management Plan) for the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Incorporation in land and resource management
children.label: B Incorporation in land and resource management
children.text: Upon its completion, the Opal Creek Management Plan shall become part of the land and resource management plan for the Willamette National Forest and supersede any conflicting provision in such land and resource management plan. Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to supersede the requirements of the Endangered Species Act [ et seq.] or the National Forest Management Act or regulations promulgated under those Acts, or any other law.16 U.S.C. 1531

children.label: i
children.text: recreation;

children.label: ii
children.text: harvesting of nontraditional forest products, such as gathering mushrooms and material to make baskets; and

children.label: iii
children.text: educational and research opportunities.

children.heading: Plan amendments
children.label: D Plan amendments
children.text: The Secretary may amend the Opal Creek Management Plan as the Secretary may determine to be necessary, consistent with the procedures and purposes of this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date of establishment of the Scenic Recreation Area, the Secretary shall review and revise the inventory of the cultural and historic resources on the public land in the Scenic Recreation Area developed pursuant to the Oregon Wilderness Act of 1984 ().Public Law 98–328

children.heading: Interpretation
children.label: B Interpretation
children.text: Interpretive activities shall be developed under the management plan in consultation with State and local historic preservation organizations and shall include a balanced and factual interpretation of the cultural, ecological, and industrial history of forestry and mining in the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: In general1So in original. No subpar. (B) has been enacted.1
children.label: A In general1So in original. No subpar. (B) has been enacted.1
i
children.text: evaluates the road network within the Scenic Recreation Area to determine which roads should be retained and which roads should be closed;

children.label: ii
children.text: provides guidelines for transportation and access consistent with this section;

children.label: iii
children.text: considers the access needs of persons with disabilities in preparing the transportation plan for the Scenic Recreation Area;

children.label: iv
children.text: allows forest road 2209 beyond the gate to the Scenic Recreation Area, as depicted on the map described in subsection (a)(2), to be used by motorized vehicles only for administrative purposes and for access by private inholders, subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may determine to be necessary; and

children.label: v
children.text: restricts construction or improvement of forest road 2209 beyond the gate to the Scenic Recreation Area to maintaining the character of the road as it existed upon , which shall not include paving or widening.November 12, 1996

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to applicable Federal and State law, the Secretary shall permit hunting and fishing in the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: The Secretary may designate zones in which, and establish periods when, no hunting or fishing shall be permitted for reasons of public safety, administration, or public use and enjoyment of the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: C Consultation
children.text: Except during an emergency, as determined by the Secretary, the Secretary shall consult with the Oregon State Department of Fish and Wildlife before issuing any regulation under this subsection.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary shall prohibit the cutting and/or selling of trees in the Scenic Reservation Area.

children.label: I
children.text: for public safety, such as to control the continued spread of a forest fire in the Scenic Recreation Area or on land adjacent to the Scenic Recreation Area;

children.label: II
children.text: for activities related to administration of the Scenic Recreation Area, consistent with the Opal Creek Management Plan; or

children.label: III
children.text: for removal of hazard trees along trails and roadways.

children.heading: Salvage sales
children.label: ii Salvage sales
children.text: The Secretary may not allow a salvage sale in the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.label: i
children.text: any form of entry, appropriation, or disposal under the public lands laws;

children.label: ii
children.text: location, entry, and patent under the mining laws; and

children.label: iii
children.text: disposition under the mineral and geothermal leasing laws.

children.label: A
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to interfere with or approve any exploration, mining, or mining-related activity in the Bornite Project Area, depicted on the map described in subsection (a)(3), conducted in accordance with applicable laws.

children.label: B
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to interfere with the ability of the Secretary to approve and issue, or deny, special use permits in connection with exploration, mining, and mining-related activities in the Bornite Project Area.

children.label: C
children.text: Motorized vehicles, roads, structures, and utilities (including but not limited to power lines and water lines) may be allowed inside the Scenic Recreation Area to serve the activities conducted on land within the Bornite Project.

children.label: D
children.text: After , no patent shall be issued for any mining claim under the general mining laws located within the Bornite Project Area.November 12, 1996

children.heading: Water impoundments
children.label: 9 Water impoundments
children.text: Notwithstanding the Federal Power Act ( et seq.), the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission may not license the construction of any dam, water conduit, reservoir, powerhouse, transmission line, or other project work in the Scenic Recreation Area, except as may be necessary to comply with the provisions of paragraph (8) with regard to the Bornite Project.16 U.S.C. 791a

children.heading: Recognition
children.label: A Recognition
children.text: Congress recognizes recreation as an appropriate use of the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Minimum levels
children.label: B Minimum levels
children.text: The management plan shall permit recreation activities at not less than the levels in existence on .November 12, 1996

children.heading: Higher levels
children.label: C Higher levels
children.text: The management plan may provide for levels of recreation use higher than the levels in existence on , if such uses are consistent with the protection of the resource values of the Scenic Recreation Area.November 12, 1996

children.heading: Public trail access
children.label: D Public trail access
children.text: The management plan may include public trail access through section 28, township 8 south, range 5 east, Willamette Meridian, to Battle Ax Creek, Opal Pool and other areas in the Opal Creek Wilderness and the Opal Creek Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Advisory council
children.label: A Advisory council
children.text: The Secretary shall consult on a periodic and regular basis with the advisory council established under subsection (e) with respect to matters relating to management of the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Public participation
children.label: B Public participation
children.text: The Secretary shall seek the views of private groups, individuals, and the public concerning the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Other agencies
children.label: C Other agencies
children.text: The Secretary shall seek the views and assistance of, and cooperate with, any other Federal, State, or local agency with any responsibility for the zoning, planning, or natural resources of the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Nonprofit agencies and organizations
children.label: D Nonprofit agencies and organizations
children.text: The Secretary shall seek the views of any nonprofit agency or organization that may contribute information or expertise about the resources and the management of the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: Not later than 90 days after the establishment of the Scenic Recreation Area, the Secretary shall establish an advisory council for the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.label: A
children.text: 1 member shall represent Marion County, Oregon, and shall be designated by the governing body of the county;

children.label: B
children.text: 1 member shall represent the State of Oregon and shall be designated by the Governor of Oregon;

children.label: C
children.text: 1 member shall represent the City of Salem, and shall be designated by the mayor of Salem, Oregon;

children.label: D
children.text: 1 member from a city within a 25-mile radius of the Opal Creek Scenic Recreation Area, to be designated by the Governor of the State of Oregon from a list of candidates provided by the mayors of the cities located within a 25-mile radius of the Opal Creek Scenic Recreation Area; and

children.label: E
children.text: not more than 9 members shall be appointed by the Secretary from among persons who, individually or through association with a national or local organization, have an interest in the administration of the Scenic Recreation Area, including, but not limited to, representatives of the timber industry, environmental organizations, the mining industry, inholders in the Opal Creek Wilderness and Scenic Recreation Area, economic development interests and Indian tribes.

children.heading: Staggered terms
children.label: 3 Staggered terms
children.text: Members of the advisory council shall serve for staggered terms of 3 years.

children.heading: Chairman
children.label: 4 Chairman
children.text: The Secretary shall designate 1 member of the advisory council as chairman.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 5 Vacancies
children.text: The Secretary shall fill a vacancy on the advisory council in the same manner as the original appointment.

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 6 Compensation
children.text: Members of the advisory council shall receive no compensation for their service on the advisory council.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the other provisions of this section, the Secretary may acquire any lands or interests in land in the Scenic Recreation Area or the Opal Creek Wilderness that the Secretary determines are needed to carry out this section.

children.heading: Public land
children.label: B Public land
children.text: Any lands or interests in land owned by a State or a political subdivision of a State may be acquired only by donation or exchange.

children.label: i
children.text: the nature of land use has changed significantly, or the landowner has demonstrated intent to change the land use significantly, from the use that existed on ; andNovember 12, 1996

children.label: ii
children.text: acquisition by the Secretary of the land or interest in land is essential to ensure use of the land or interest in land in accordance with the purposes of this title or the management plan prepared under subsection (d)(2).3So in original. Probably should be “section”.3

children.heading: Construction
children.label: D Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to enhance or diminish the condemnation authority available to the Secretary outside the boundaries of the Opal Creek Wilderness or the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: Environmental response actions and cost recovery
children.label: 2 Environmental response actions and cost recovery

children.heading: Response actions
children.label: A Response actions
children.text: Nothing in this section shall limit the authority of the Secretary or a responsible party to conduct an environmental response action in the Scenic Recreation Area in connection with the release, threatened release, or cleanup of a hazardous substance, pollutant, or contaminant, including a response action conducted under the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act of 1980 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 9601

children.heading: Liability
children.label: B Liability
children.text: Nothing in this section shall limit the authority of the Secretary or a responsible party to recover costs related to the release, threatened release, or cleanup of any hazardous substance or pollutant or contaminant in the Scenic Recreation Area.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: As soon as practicable after , the Secretary shall file a map and a boundary description for the Opal Creek Wilderness and for the Scenic Recreation Area with the Committee on Resources of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate.November 12, 1996

children.heading: Force and effect
children.label: B Force and effect
children.text: The boundary description and map shall have the same force and effect as if the description and map were included in this section, except that the Secretary may correct clerical and typographical errors in the boundary description and map.

children.heading: Availability
children.label: C Availability
children.text: The map and boundary description shall be on file and available for public inspection in the Office of the Chief of the Forest Service, Department of Agriculture.

children.heading: Savings provision
children.label: 4 Savings provision
children.text: Nothing in this section shall interfere with activity for which a special use permit has been issued, has not been revoked, and has not expired, before , subject to the terms of the permit.November 12, 1996

children.heading: Authorization
children.label: 1 Authorization
children.text: Notwithstanding any other law, if the Rosboro Lumber Company (referred to in this subsection as “Rosboro”) offers and conveys marketable title to the United States to the land described in paragraph (2), the Secretary of Agriculture shall convey all right, title and interest held by the United States to sufficient lands described in paragraph (3) to Rosboro, in the order in which they appear in this subsection, as necessary to satisfy the equal value requirements of paragraph (4).

children.heading: Land to be offered by Rosboro
children.label: 2 Land to be offered by Rosboro
children.text: The land referred to in paragraph (1) as the land to be offered by Rosboro shall comprise Section 36, Township 8 South, Range 4 East, Willamette Meridian.

children.heading: Land to be conveyed by the United States
children.label: 3 Land to be conveyed by the United States
A
children.text: Section 5, Township 17 South, Range 4 East, Lot 7 (37.63 acres);

children.label: B
children.text: Section 2, Township 17 South, Range 4 East, Lot 3 (29.28 acres);

children.label: C
children.text: Section 13, Township 17 South, Range 4 East, S½SE¼ (80 acres);

children.label: D
children.text: Section 2, Township 17 South, Range 4 East, SW¼SW¼ (40 acres);

children.label: E
children.text: Section 2, Township 17 South, Range 4 East, NW¼SE¼ (40 acres);

children.label: F
children.text: Section 8, Township 17 South, Range 4 East, SE¼SW¼ (40 acres);

children.label: G
children.text: Section 11, Township 17 South, Range 4 East, W½NW¼ (80 acres);4So in original. The semicolon probably should be a period.4

children.heading: Equal value
children.label: 4 Equal value
children.text: The land and interests in land exchanged under this subsection shall be of equal market value as determined by nationally recognized appraisal standards, including, to the extent appropriate, the Uniform Standards for Federal Land Acquisition, the Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice, or shall be equalized by way of payment of cash pursuant to the provisions of , and other applicable law. The appraisal shall consider access costs for the parcels involved.section 1716(d) of title 43

children.label: A
children.text: The exchange directed by this subsection shall be consummated not later than 120 days after the date Rosboro offers and conveys the property described in paragraph (2) to the United States.

children.label: B
children.text: The authority provided by this subsection shall lapse of Rosboro fails to offer the land described in paragraph (2) within 2 years after .5So in original. Probably should be “if”.5November 12, 1996

children.heading: Challenge
children.label: 6 Challenge
children.text: Rosboro shall have the right to challenge in the United States District Court for the District of Oregon a determination of marketability under paragraph (1) and a determination of value for the lands described in paragraphs (2) and (3) by the Secretary of Agriculture. The court shall have the authority to order the Secretary to complete the transaction contemplated in this subsection.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 7 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection.

children.heading: Economic development plan
children.label: 1 Economic development plan
children.text: As a condition for receiving funding under paragraph (2), the State of Oregon, in consultation with Marion County, Oregon, and the Secretary of Agriculture, shall develop a plan for economic development projects for which grants under this subsection may be used in a manner consistent with this section and to benefit local communities in the vicinity of the Opal Creek area. Such plan shall be based on an economic opportunity study and other appropriate information.

children.heading: Funds provided to the States for grants
children.label: 2 Funds provided to the States for grants
children.text: Upon completion of the Opal Creek Management Plan, and receipt of the plan referred to in paragraph (1), the Secretary shall provide, subject to appropriations, $15,000,000 to the State of Oregon. Such funds shall be used to make grants or loans for economic development projects that further the purposes of this section and benefit the local communities in the vicinity of the Opal Creek area.

children.label: A
children.text: prepare and provide the Secretary and Congress with an annual report on the use of the funds made available under this subsection;

children.label: B
children.text: make available to the Secretary and to Congress, upon request, all accounts, financial records, and other information related to grants and loans made available pursuant to this subsection; and

children.label: C
children.text: as loans are repaid, make additional grants and loans with the money made available for obligation by such repayments.
